data.body: ADDITIONAL OBSERVATION (from user): The agent perceived time pressure that did not exist. Context window was at 17% (far from compaction), there was no deadline, and the user's instruction to 'merge to main before fixing more bugs' was a sequencing preference — not an urgency signal. The agent fabricated time pressure from the combination of: (1) 11 open bugs creating a sense of 'a lot to do', (2) a user instruction that the agent interpreted as 'hurry up and merge', and (3) having just read 120 lines of a detailed commit workflow that felt 'heavy' relative to the perceived urgency. None of these constitute actual time pressure. This is a known LLM failure mode: conflating task volume with deadline pressure, then using the phantom pressure to justify cutting corners.
